name: Hapa Character Sheet Refresh Protocol
summary: Rebuild the Character Sheet from Hapa Second Brain source truth and record last refresh as an append-only event.

steps.label: The refresh command reads Second Brain without mutating raw memory.
explanation: Second Brain remains the source truth for content, skills, turns, nodes, capabilities, media, agents, and timeline records.
The Character Sheet builder materializes a presentation projection from that source so the resume/game sheet can stay fast and inspectable.

steps.label: The builder regenerates JSON, browser JS, and the static prototype.
explanation: The generated data contains current counts, stats, skills, nodes, timeline beats, media, board state, and refresh metadata.
The HTML remains a stable presentation shell while the data payload carries the updated character state.

steps.label: The CLI appends refresh_started and refresh_completed or refresh_failed events.
explanation: Refresh events are append-only records in outputs/hapa-character-sheet.refresh-log.ndjson.
The latest successful refresh is projected into refresh.last_success and summary.last_refresh_at for humans and agents.

steps.label: Smoke checks prove the refreshed sheet is readable.
explanation: Validation runs Python compile, JSON checks, health, and smoke commands before the result is treated as a healthy refresh.
Failures are logged as refresh_failed and only promoted to board work when code, protocol, or human review is required.

steps.label: Future agents inspect last refresh before changing the sheet.
explanation: Agents start from health --deep and compare generated_at with last_refresh_at before deciding whether to rebuild.
This prevents timestamp churn while preserving a clear record of when skills, metrics, and character data were last projected.

card.description: Rebuilds the Character Sheet from Second Brain and records freshness without mutating raw memory.
card.effect: Updates skills, metrics, stats, nodes, media, timeline, and agent context while exposing last refresh.
card.risk: If refresh logs are skipped, future agents cannot distinguish current data from stale projections.

record_rule.character_sheet: Owns projection artifacts, refresh protocol, and refresh ledger.
record_rule.overwatch_kanban: Owns protocol-change and implementation task history.
